data.content:
Implementation plan for first-class knot acceptance:

1. Core schema and in-memory model.
Update `src/db.rs` with a new schema migration that adds `knot_hot.acceptance TEXT`, then thread that field through `KnotCacheRecord`, `UpsertKnotHot`, row decoding, insert/update SQL, and every `UpsertKnotHot` callsite in `src/app.rs`, `src/sync/apply.rs`, `src/snapshots.rs`, and test helpers. Add `acceptance: Option<String>` to `KnotView`, `CreateKnotOptions`, and `UpdateKnotPatch`. Keep acceptance independent from `description`/`body`; do not mirror it into `body`.

2. Write path and event propagation.
Add `FullEventKind::KnotAcceptanceSet` in `src/events/mod.rs`. In `src/cli.rs`, add `--acceptance` to `kno new` and `kno update`. In `src/write_queue.rs` and `src/write_dispatch.rs`, carry acceptance through `NewOperation` and `UpdateOperation`, and make sure the create path uses `CreateKnotOptions` plus `create_knot_with_options`, not only `App::create_knot`. In `src/app.rs`, emit `knot.acceptance_set` during create/update when appropriate, update `UpdateKnotPatch::has_changes`, and teach rehydration to apply the new event. In `src/sync/apply.rs`, apply `knot.acceptance_set` into hot-cache records during sync.

3. Read surfaces and prompts.
Render knot acceptance in `src/prompt.rs` as an `## Acceptance Criteria` section so claim/skill prompts expose knot-level acceptance during execution. Also surface it in `KnotView` JSON and in human-readable `kno show` output via `src/ui.rs`; once `KnotView` has the field, JSON show will follow automatically from serde. This answers the open plan-review question: yes, `src/ui.rs` should show first-class acceptance alongside the other first-class knot fields.

4. Foolery integration.
In `../foolery/src/lib/knots.ts`, add acceptance to `KnotRecord`, `newKnot(...)` options, and `KnotUpdateInput`, and serialize it with `--acceptance` on both create and update. In `../foolery/src/lib/backends/knots-backend.ts`, remove the acceptance-note shim (`Acceptance Criteria:` marker helpers and secondary update calls), use the native knot acceptance field in `create`, `update`, and `toBeat()`, and stop filtering acceptance out of visible notes because it will no longer be encoded as a note.

5. Tests, child mapping, and risks.
Keep the existing child hierarchy; no new child knots are needed. Map work as follows: `e071` schema/data model and all `UpsertKnotHot` callsites, `2bc3` event kind plus rehydration/sync, `09f2` CLI flags plus queued write path plus `create_knot_with_options`, `93c9` prompt/read surfaces including `src/ui.rs`, `2aa3` foolery CLI wrapper, `ed42` foolery backend migration off the note shim, and `28b1` coverage across both repos. Test coverage should include Rust CLI parsing/dispatch, event kind string coverage, sync apply/rehydration, prompt rendering, human-readable show rendering, and TypeScript backend contract/coverage tests for acceptance round-trip and single-write create/update behavior. Main risks are empty-string clear semantics on update, missing `UpsertKnotHot` callsites after the new field lands, and keeping knot acceptance distinct from workflow prompt acceptance so both can render without confusion.

Complexity: medium-high because the change crosses schema, event, queued-write, sync, prompt, UI, and TypeScript backend boundaries, but it is bounded and already decomposed into the existing child knots.
